headline: Survey at 11.6% completion; strong engagement in 3 regions masks severe geographic gaps.
collection_status: 157 of 1,350 responses collected (11.6% progress). Lindi dominates with 40.8% of submissions; 3 regions remain inactive. Current pace suggests 6-9 month timeline to target if momentum accelerates significantly.
key_findings: Teacher representation strong (75 respondents, 47.8%) but head teachers and education officials underrepresented; WEO, RC, ARCs, DEOs collectively only 26 responses (16.6%).
Expansion priorities clear: 'Expand KF' (58 votes, 37%) and new programs (35 votes, 22%) dominate; policy alignment (32 votes) signals demand for government integration.
Scale readiness score 2.7/5 indicates significant operational and systemic barriers; partner satisfaction 4.5/5 suggests relationship strength masks implementation challenges.
Mtwara, Songwe, Singida severely underrepresented (13 combined responses, 8.3%); Ruvuma and Kagera absent entirely from 10-region target.
flags: Geographic concentration risk: Lindi-Tanga-Kigoma account for 86.6% of responses; 7 regions inactive or critically underfunded.
Systemic readiness misalignment: High satisfaction (4.5/5) paired with low scale readiness (2.7/5) suggests stakeholders underestimate implementation complexity.
Official stakeholder gap: Only 21 responses from district/regional education officials (13.4%); policy recommendations lack institutional validation.
recommendation: Immediately launch targeted push in Mtwara, Songwe, Singida, and absent regions with localized outreach to WEOs/REOs. Conduct rapid 1-on-1 interviews with 15-20 education officials to surface scale barriers and reframe readiness conversation before month-end.
